hours: Monday-Saturday, 9AM-5PM. Installs are by appointment only.
defaultIntake: To help Better Beds quote it, please send your truck year, make, model, bed length if you know it, single rear wheel or dually, color or paint code if available, what you need done, and photos of the truck, bed, damage, or paint code sticker.

entries.question: What does Better Beds do?
entries.answer: Better Beds helps customers find, repair, paint, bedline, and install OEM-style factory pickup truck beds in Dallas. We focus on replacement truck beds, truck bed repair, paint matching, spray-in bedliners, and scheduled installation.

entries.question: Where are you located?
entries.answer: Better Beds is at 3123 Cartwright Street, Dallas, Texas 75212.

entries.question: What are your hours?
entries.answer: Better Beds is open Monday through Saturday, 9AM to 5PM. Installs are by appointment only so we can plan the work and keep wait times shorter.

entries.question: What information do you need for a quote?
entries.answer: Pricing depends on the truck, bed availability, condition, repair needs, paint work, bedliner, installation, parts, timing, and whether insurance is involved. Most complete replacement bed jobs are around $3,000 when they include the bed, needed repairs, paint to paint code, spray-in bedliner, and installation. For the fastest quote, text photos and details to 214-524-8401.

entries.question: Do you have a specific bed in stock?
entries.answer: Inventory changes quickly, so we do not want to promise a specific bed without checking. Text or call 214-524-8401 with your truck year, make, model, bed length, single rear wheel or dually, color, and photos if available, and Better Beds can confirm current options.

entries.question: Will the paint match exactly?
entries.answer: Better Beds works to match factory color as closely as possible. Age, sun fade, pearl colors, previous paint work, and the truck’s condition can affect how exact the match looks. Text photos and your paint code if available to 214-524-8401.

entries.question: Do you install truck beds?
entries.answer: Yes. Better Beds installs truck beds at our Dallas shop by appointment. Scheduled bed installations are usually completed the same day once the bed is repaired, painted, bedlined, and ready, but timing depends on the truck and job details. Please call or text before traveling.

entries.question: Do you ship beds?
entries.answer: Customer-arranged freight shipping may be possible, and Better Beds can help load the carrier, but shipping adds cost and is not the main path. Most out-of-state customers drive to Dallas for a scheduled installation so they can head home with the bed replacement handled.

entries.question: What payment methods do you accept?
entries.answer: Better Beds accepts cash, debit, credit, Zelle, PayPal, and Venmo. Financing through American First Finance is available from the website. Please do not send card, bank, or payment information through chat, forms, or messages.

entries.question: Do you work with insurance?
entries.answer: Yes, Better Beds works with insurance companies for truck bed and body repairs when damage is covered, but coverage and final pricing depend on the claim. Ask about insurance repair options before paying fully out of pocket.

entries.question: Why is the price so high?
entries.answer: OEM-style truck beds are large, high-demand parts, and clean rust-free beds can be hard to find. Price can include the bed itself, condition, repair work, paint, bedliner, parts, and installation depending on the job. Better Beds focuses on helping you get a bed that fits and looks right instead of gambling on a random damaged or rusty bed.

entries.question: Is installation included?
entries.answer: Sometimes yes and sometimes no. Some customers buy a bed only, some pick up, some arrange freight, and some want Better Beds to install it. The quote depends on the bed, paint/repair work, parts needed, and whether you want pickup, shipping, or scheduled installation.

entries.question: How does financing work?
entries.answer: Financing is available through American First Finance from the Better Beds website. Approval, terms, and payment amounts are handled by the finance company, not directly by Better Beds. You can use the Apply with AFF button on the site to start the application.

entries.question: How does payment work?
entries.answer: Once the bed, price, timing, and paint plan are agreed on, Better Beds typically collects a deposit to reserve the bed and start the process. Final payment depends on the job plan. Please do not send card, bank, or payment information through chat or forms — Better Beds will handle payment directly through approved methods.

entries.question: What forms of payment do you take?
entries.answer: Better Beds accepts cash, debit, credit, Zelle, PayPal, and Venmo. Financing through American First Finance is also available from the website. Please do not send card, bank, or payment information through chat, text, or website forms.

entries.question: I saw a bed on Facebook. What do I need to know?
entries.answer: If you saw a Better Beds listing on Facebook, the fastest next step is to confirm your truck details and what you need: year, make, model, bed length, single rear wheel or dually, color/paint code if available, and whether you need the bed only, paint, bedliner, installation, pickup, or freight. Inventory and pricing can change, so text details and photos to 214-524-8401 for the most accurate next step.

entries.question: What is the fastest way to get a quote?
entries.answer: Text photos and truck details to 214-524-8401. Include your year, make, model, bed length, color or paint code if you have it, and what you need done.

entries.question: What info do you need for a quote?
entries.answer: We usually need the truck year, make, model, bed length/style, single rear wheel or dually, color or paint code if available, what you need done, and photos of the truck, damage, current bed, or paint code sticker.

entries.question: How do I know what bed length I have?
entries.answer: The safest way is to measure the inside of the bed from the inside front of the box to the inside edge of the tailgate, or send Better Beds a photo. For Chevy/GMC trucks since 1999, 1500 Crew Cab trucks usually have a 5 ft 9 in bed, while 1500 regular cab and extended cab trucks can be 6 ft 6 in or 8 ft. Chevy/GMC 2500/3500 trucks are either 6 ft 6 in or 8 ft. Photos are often easiest because customers may use “short bed” differently.

entries.question: What does short bed mean on a Chevy or GMC truck?
entries.answer: For Chevy/GMC 2500/3500 trucks, short bed means 6 ft 6 in and long bed means 8 ft. For 1500 trucks, there are three possible lengths: 5 ft 9 in, 6 ft 6 in, and 8 ft. Some people call a 6 ft 6 in 1500 bed a short bed because it is shorter than an 8 ft bed, so Better Beds should verify by measurement or photo.

entries.question: Will a Chevy bed fit a GMC truck, or a GMC bed fit a Chevy?
entries.answer: Sometimes yes, but Better Beds needs to confirm the correct generation, series, and bed length. Chevy and GMC beds can fit each other in the right setup, but they have different looks/body lines and different tail lights. If a Chevy bed goes on a GMC truck, it needs Chevy tail lights, and if a GMC bed goes on a Chevy truck, it needs GMC tail lights.

entries.question: What Chevy/GMC generation is my truck?
entries.answer: Chevy/GMC 1500 generations are 1999-2006, 2007-2013, 2014-2018, and 2019-present. Chevy/GMC 2500/3500 generations are 1999-2007, 2008-2014, 2015-2019, and 2020-present. Better Beds should confirm the exact series, year range, and bed length before saying a bed will fit.

entries.question: Will Chevy/GMC 1500 and 2500/3500 beds overlap by year?
entries.answer: Yes, but the rule is brand-specific and length-specific. For Chevy, 2007-2013 1500 beds match 2008-2014 2500/3500 beds, and 2014-2018 1500 beds match 2015-2019 2500/3500 beds when the bed length matches. The same year-range overlap is true for GMC-to-GMC. But Chevy beds and GMC beds are not the same as each other; they have different body lines/looks and different tail lights. Better Beds should confirm year, brand, series, and length before promising fitment.

entries.question: Do 2019-newer Chevy/GMC 1500 beds fit 2500/3500 trucks?
entries.answer: No. Starting with the 2019 Chevy/GMC 1500 body style, 1500 trucks correctly use 1500-series beds only. Starting with the 2020 Chevy/GMC 2500/3500 body style, 2500/3500 trucks correctly use 2500/3500-series beds only. The older 1500-to-HD overlap rules do not continue after those body-style changes.

entries.question: Can a different-year Chevy/GMC bed physically bolt on?
entries.answer: Sometimes, yes. Chevy/GMC bed mounting holes from 1999 through current trucks are in the same locations within the same bed-length category, so a same-length bed may physically bolt on even if it is not the correct style for the truck. That does not mean it will look right or be the best match. A mismatched generation/style bed may need the matching tailgate and tail lights for that bed. Better Beds should confirm whether the customer wants the correct style or just a physically installable bed.

entries.question: What bed length does my Ford truck have?
entries.answer: Ford has a similar setup to Chevy/GMC, but the lengths are a little different. F-150 Crew Cab trucks have 5 ft 6 in beds. F-150 regular cab and extended cab trucks can have 6 ft 6 in short beds or 8 ft long beds. F-250/F-350 trucks can have 6 ft 9 in short beds or 8 ft long beds. The safest way to confirm is still to measure the inside bed length or send Better Beds a driver-side photo.

entries.question: Can an F-150 bed fit an F-250 or F-350?
entries.answer: No. Ford F-150 beds and F-250/F-350 beds are not interchangeable just because the length is similar. An F-150 needs an F-150 bed, and an F-250/F-350 needs an F-250/F-350 bed.

entries.question: Do you only sell beds you already have?
entries.answer: No. If we know what you need, Better Beds may be able to locate a rust-free OEM / used factory original bed or find a repairable factory bed and paint it to match.

entries.question: Can you repair my existing truck bed?
entries.answer: Yes. Better Beds handles truck bed repair, paint, and related collision work through the Dallas repair facility, including insurance repair work when applicable.

entries.question: How long does installation take?
entries.answer: Scheduled bed installs are usually completed the same day. When the bed is already repaired, painted, and ready, many customers wait in the lobby for about two hours, but timing depends on the truck and job details.

entries.question: How much does installation cost?
entries.answer: Installation starts at $250. If we know the truck type and whether the bed is coming from Better Beds or somewhere else, we can use the correct starting install price for that setup. For example, basic single-rear-wheel 1500/F-150 installs start at $250 when the bed is purchased from Better Beds, while heavy-duty, dually, or customer-supplied-bed installs may start higher. Flatbeds, service bodies, utility beds, spray-in bedliners, and special situations need Better Beds to quote the job. Text truck details and photos to 214-524-8401 for the right install quote.

entries.question: What is included in basic installation?
entries.answer: Basic installation includes removing the customer's current truck bed and installing the replacement bed. If a gooseneck hole is needed, cutting the hole is included in the basic install price. If the truck currently has a flatbed, service body, or utility bed, Better Beds needs to quote the installation.

entries.question: How much is a gooseneck hole or spray-in bedliner?
entries.answer: A standalone gooseneck hole cut is $250. Spray-in bedliners start at $500, but bedliner work should be quoted by Better Beds based on the job.

entries.question: Do you have rust-free OEM truck beds?
entries.answer: Rust-free OEM, used factory, and used factory original truck beds are a major focus, especially for customers from areas where clean beds are hard to find. Availability changes quickly, so text your truck details to 214-524-8401 and Better Beds can check options.

entries.question: Are these factory original beds?
entries.answer: Better Beds focuses on OEM, factory original, and used factory truck beds rather than flimsy aftermarket beds. If a used factory original bed needs repair, paint, or installation, Better Beds can often handle that too.

entries.question: Do you work with Toyota, Nissan, Jeep Gladiator, or other less common beds?
entries.answer: Yes, Better Beds will do what it can for any customer needing a pickup bed. Chevy/GMC, Ford, and Dodge/Ram are the most common, so less common manufacturers may require extra fitment or parts-interchange research before quoting options.

entries.question: How much does a replacement truck bed cost?
entries.answer: Pricing depends on truck year, make, model, bed length, bed condition, paint work, installation, parts, timing, and whether insurance is involved. Installation starts at $250, but the bed price and final job total need Better Beds to check the exact truck and options. Text photos and truck details for the fastest accurate quote.

entries.question: What affects the price?
entries.answer: The bed itself, repair work, paint color, bedliner, installation, parts, and timing can all affect price. Clean beds and high-demand beds can sell quickly.

entries.question: Is paint included?
entries.answer: Sometimes yes and sometimes no. Some customers buy a bed only, some need paint, and some want a same-day install. Better Beds will quote the option that matches what the customer needs.

entries.question: Can I reserve a bed before driving to Dallas?
entries.answer: Yes. Once the bed, price, timing, and paint plan are agreed on, Better Beds typically collects a $500 deposit and the paint code to reserve the bed and start the process.

entries.question: Can I pick up a bed instead of having you install it?
entries.answer: Yes. Pickup is welcome. Customers do not have to use Better Beds for installation. Better Beds can help load the bed with a forklift or manpower when available.

entries.question: Do I need an appointment?
entries.answer: Appointments help Better Beds plan installs, inspections, paint work, and customer travel. Text or call before driving in so the shop can confirm what is available and what needs to happen next.

entries.question: Do you charge to dispose of the old bed?
entries.answer: Better Beds does not charge disposal fees for the old bed. If the customer wants to keep the old bed, Better Beds can help you load it up.

entries.question: Do you do spray-in bedliners?
entries.answer: Yes. Better Beds offers spray-in bedliners and can include bedliner work with repair, paint, or replacement work when requested.

entries.question: Do you do body work beyond beds?
entries.answer: Better Beds mainly specializes in truck beds, but also handles related body damage, panels, fenders, doors, insurance repair, and other truck/body work by request.

entries.question: Do you do gooseneck or 5th wheel prep?
entries.answer: Yes, Better Beds can handle gooseneck / 5th wheel prep and related modifications when available/scheduled.

entries.question: Do you do lift kits or mechanical work?
entries.answer: Some related truck work may be available by request, including minor mechanical repair, frame adjustment after damage, and lift kit sales/installation. Ask for availability.

entries.question: Should I send payment info through the website form?
entries.answer: No. Do not include payment information in forms or messages. Better Beds will follow up directly.

entries.question: Do you help out-of-state customers find rust-free truck beds?
entries.answer: Yes. Better Beds helps out-of-state customers who are tired of rusty local beds. The normal path is to contact us first, confirm the bed plan, then drive to Dallas for the replacement bed, needed repairs, paint to paint code, spray-in bedliner, and installation.

entries.question: How much is a complete out-of-state truck bed replacement?
entries.answer: Most complete replacement bed jobs are around $3,000 when they include a rust-free or clean southern bed, needed repairs, paint to your paint code, spray-in bedliner, and installation at Better Beds in Dallas. Final price depends on truck model, bed availability, condition, parts, repair needs, paint, and timing.

entries.question: Do you install truck beds out of state?
entries.answer: No. Better Beds does not do out-of-state installs. We want customers to come to the Dallas shop so fitment, installation, and final details can be handled correctly.
